source_name: Code4rena Reserve Protocol Invitational Findings and Analysis Report (2023-07)
finding_title: CurveStableMetapoolCollateral.tryPrice returns a huge but valid high price when the price oracle of pairedToken is timeout
summary: The CurveStableMetapoolCollateral is intended for 2-fiattoken stable metapools. The metapoolToken coin0 is pairedToken and the coin1 is lpToken, e.g. 3CRV. And the config.chainlinkFeed should be set for paired token.
root_cause: CurveStableMetapoolCollateral accepts an extreme but valid price when pairedToken oracle data is timed out
broken_invariants: collateral tryPrice must reject or bound composed prices when any paired token oracle is stale
timeout handling in one leg of a metapool must not produce a huge still-valid price
oracle freshness must propagate through composed Curve metapool pricing
attack_preconditions: the affected Reserve collateral/plugin/wrapper is deployed or configured in an RToken basket
the report-described oracle, reward, wrapper, or integration edge case is reachable in the audited code version
attacker_capability: external user, trader, or protocol participant able to trigger the affected pricing/trading/reward/withdrawal flow
trigger_actions: execute or wait for the report-described state transition
observe mispricing, reward misallocation, fund lock, or availability failure

impact.description: The CurveStableMetapoolCollateral is intended for 2-fiattoken stable metapools. The metapoolToken coin0 is pairedToken and the coin1 is lpToken, e.g. 3CRV. And the config.chainlinkFeed should be set for paired token.

why_not_higher: final C4 severity is Medium; impact, preconditions, or scope are bounded relative to High
why_not_lower: included as a validated High/Medium C4 finding with concrete affected code paths and impact narrative
common_false_positive_angles: verify the exact collateral plugin/wrapper is in scope and configured
check whether oracle freshness, decimals, or external integration assumptions differ in the current target
confirm the issue affects value movement, redemption availability, or reward ownership rather than a harmless view mismatch
mitigations: Don’t try catch the this.tryPairedPrice() in the CurveStableMetapoolCollateral.tryPrice, if it failed, just let the whole tryPrice function revert, the caller, for example refresh(), can catch the error. ### Assessed type Context
